title: Doorway / Duplication Risk Audit — Long-Tail Pages
principle: Google's doorway page policy: Pages that are substantially similar, primarily funnel users to another page, or exist only to capture search traffic with thin differentiation are at risk.

risk_classification.SAFE: Page is meaningfully unique. Has distinct value beyond funneling. Can be published indexable.
risk_classification.NEEDS_MORE_UNIQUE_VALUE: Page has unique calculation but explanation/context is too thin. Fix before publishing.
risk_classification.CANNIBALIZATION_RISK: Page targets same or overlapping keywords as parent money page or sibling. Risk of diluting ranking signals.
risk_classification.DOORWAY_RISK: Page is substantially similar to siblings, primarily funnels to parent calculator, or adds minimal unique value. HIGH RISK of Google penalty.
risk_classification.REJECT: Page is clearly a doorway, duplicate, or number-swap variant. DO NOT PUBLISH.

examples: Same template, different rate. Google may see these as doorway pages.
number_swap_variants.mitigation: Only publish if each variant has SUBSTANTIALLY unique explanation referencing specific real-world scenarios. E.g., '8% is an excellent rate for good credit' vs '12% is an average rate for fair credit' — and each page must have 3+ unique content blocks.
number_swap_variants.max_variants_per_base: 3-5 variants per loan amount (different rates/terms) with strong differentiation. Do NOT publish all 1,620 loan variants.

funnel_pages.description: Pages whose primary purpose is to show a number and then push users to the parent calculator
funnel_pages.detection: If above-fold content is calculation result + CTA to parent calculator, with minimal unique content below, this is a doorway pattern
funnel_pages.mitigation: Above-fold must include unique explanation/comparison/warning. CTA to parent calculator must be secondary to standalone value.

near_duplicate_templates.description: Pages built from the same template where only the numbers change
near_duplicate_templates.examples: All loan payment pages using identical section structure with swapped numbers
near_duplicate_templates.mitigation: Each page must have at least 3 unique content sections not present in siblings. Must use different structural approaches (e.g., one page compares to auto loans, another to personal loans, another to student loans).

parent_cannibalization.description: Long-tail page may outrank parent money page for related head terms
parent_cannibalization.mitigation: Long-tail pages must target SPECIFIC long-tail queries only. Do not optimize for head terms. Parent page must remain the strongest page for the core calculator term.

batch_1_25_pages_audit.SAFE_0: 0 pages classified SAFE — all need additional unique value
batch_1_25_pages_audit.NEEDS_MORE_UNIQUE_VALUE_1: loan-20000-at-8-percent-5-years — strong demand (GSC), near 90 score. Fix: add rich comparison table, real-world context, credit score guidance.
batch_1_25_pages_audit.DOORWAY_RISK_24: 24 pages show number-swap or template-duplication risk at current quality level
batch_1_25_pages_audit.overall_assessment: Cannot publish any of the 25 proposed pages as-is. All need significant unique content enhancement to pass 90/100 and avoid doorway risk.

structural_fix_for_batch_1.reduce_variants: Select 3-5 STRONGEST variants (highest demand + most differentiated). Do not publish 25 near-identical pages.
per_page_unique_elements: Unique real-world scenario tying numbers to actual financial situations
Comparison table with 2-3 different scenarios (higher/lower amount, different term)
Credit score guidance specific to this rate tier
Total cost breakdown specific to this calculation
Warning or tip unique to this loan size/rate/term
Related scenario links that are contextually relevant (not auto-generated)

ongoing_audit_requirement: Every proposed batch must pass doorway audit BEFORE quality scoring. Pages flagged as DOORWAY_RISK are auto-rejected regardless of other scores.
